questions.question: In "Do you work here?", the auxiliary is {a1}.
Do you like tea? Yes, I {a1}.
A yes/no question should not begin with {a1}.
A yes/no question expects the answer yes or {a1}.
In yes/no questions, the auxiliary is usually before the {a1}.
In "Does Tom drive?", the subject is {a1}.
Choose the yes/no opener: {a1} she live here?
The first word in "Are they ready?" is {a1}.
In "Do they live here?", the subject is {a1}.
In "Did they leave?", the auxiliary comes before {a1}.
Choose the yes/no opener: {a1} they ready?
The main verb after did stays in the {a1} form.
In "Was he late?", the auxiliary is {a1}.
In "Are you listening?", the auxiliary is {a1}.
Choose the yes/no opener: {a1} he call yesterday?
With to be, we use {a1} instead of do-support.
In "Will you join us?", the subject is {a1}.
In "Has she replied?", the auxiliary is {a1}.
{a1} the clients accept the offer last week?
Choose the yes/no opener: {a1} you ever worked abroad?
With modals, we put the modal before the {a1}.
In "Have they arrived?", the subject is {a1}.
Have you checked it? No, I {a1}.
{a1} the contract be renewed next month?
In "Could we postpone it?", the modal auxiliary is {a1}.
{a1} the witnesses present during the hearing?
Choose the yes/no opener: {a1} the policy change soon?
Correct form: {a1} the proposal have been approved?
In perfect tense questions, have/has/had comes before the {a1}.
In "Must we disclose this?", the auxiliary is {a1}.

saved_test.name: Yes / No Questions (All levels V3)
saved_test.description: Standard V3 package for Yes / No Questions across A1-C2.
